title: pilea trianthmoides var. microphylla
intro: Pilea trianthmoides var. microphylla, also known as the dwarf false buttonweed, is a plant species belonging to the family Urticaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds. The health benefits of Pilea trianthmoides var. microphylla include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions.
